column5: Q1
Task
column15: Escalation Trigger

column5: Wipe down pump/baseplate/floor; remove standing water, oil, and grease.
column15: Escalate if recurring leak, pooling liquid, or unsafe walking surface.

column5: Clean coupling guard vents (if present).
column15: Escalate if vents are damaged or guard is loose/missing.

column5: Clean around seal flush lines/tubing; ensure no kinks or leaks.
column15: Escalate if leak persists or tubing is damaged.

column5: Confirm suction/discharge valves are in correct operating position.
column15: Escalate if abnormal throttling, blocked discharge, or deadheading risk is observed.

column5: Verify minimum flow/recirculation line status (if installed).
column15: Escalate if minimum flow cannot be maintained.

column5: For packed pumps, adjust gland evenly to achieve controlled drips.
column15: Escalate if leakage cannot be controlled or packing overheats.

column5: Check seal pot/support level or pressure within normal range.
column15: Escalate if low level/pressure is not recoverable.

column5: Verify coupling guard is installed and fastened; check for contact marks.
column15: Escalate if guard is missing, loose, or rubbing.

column5: Check for abnormal odors near motor and bearing housing.
column15: Escalate immediately for burning smell.

column5: Confirm access is safe: no trip hazards, missing grating, handrail issues, or pooled liquids.
column15: Escalate if unsafe access is observed.

column5: Listen for abnormal noise (bearing, seal, cavitation, looseness).
column15: Escalate for new abnormal noise.

column5: Feel vibration at bearing housings and check for visible movement.
column15: Escalate for sudden increase or visible movement.

column5: Check seal leakage rate and location.
column15: Escalate for stream leak or fast-rising leakage.

column5: Measure bearing housing temperatures (DE/NDE) by IR and trend vs baseline.
column15: Escalate if temperature is high or rapidly rising.

column5: Record suction/discharge pressure and flow vs baseline.
column15: Escalate if pressure/flow deviates materially from baseline.

column5: Record motor current or kW vs baseline.
column15: Escalate if load is abnormally high or low.

column5: Inspect for leaks at casing joints, drains, bearing housing seals, and flush lines.
column15: Escalate for active drip/stream leaks.

column5: Tighten minor fasteners on guards, nameplates, and instrument brackets.
column15: Escalate if repeated loosening is observed.

column5: Replace damaged or loose coupling guard fasteners/covers like-for-like.
column15: Escalate if guard integrity cannot be restored.

column5: Clear minor blockage on external seal leak-off drain line where accessible.
column15: Escalate if blockage recurs or cannot be cleared.

column5: Listen for abnormal noise (gear whine, bearing rumble, knocking, pulsation roar).
column15: Escalate for new knocking, contact noise, or severe rumble.

column5: Feel vibration at bearing housings/casing; check for movement or walking.
column15: Escalate for increased vibration or visible movement.

column5: Check oil level and scan for oil leaks.
column15: Escalate for low oil or active leak.

column5: Measure bearing housing/casing temperatures by IR and trend vs baseline.
column15: Escalate if temperature is high or trending upward.

column5: Record discharge pressure and discharge temperature vs baseline.
column15: Escalate if pressure/temperature deviates abnormally.

column5: For screw blowers, check separator indicator/DP and discharge temperature trend.
column15: Escalate for high restriction or high discharge temperature.

column5: For turbo blowers, watch for vibration alarms and bearing temperature alarms.
column15: Escalate immediately for any active alarm.

column5: Tighten minor fasteners on guards, filter housing clamps, and instrument brackets.
column15: Escalate if loosening recurs.

column5: Replace damaged filter housing indicator lens/label like-for-like.
column15: Escalate if indicator remains unreadable.

column5: Clear minor external air leaks at flexible connector clamps.
column15: Escalate if leakage persists.

column5: For screw blowers, drain condensate and confirm auto-drain works.
column15: Escalate if auto-drain fails.

column5: For turbo blowers, clean/restore DP ports or sensing lines if accessible.
column15: Escalate if readings remain erratic.

column5: Check barriers, locks, and warning signage on MV/LV rooms and yard gates.
column15: Escalate for broken locks, open gates, or missing signage.

column5: Listen for abnormal arcing, buzzing, or crackling sounds near panels or bushings.
column15: Escalate immediately for any abnormal electrical sound.

column5: Smell check for burning insulation or ozone near switchgear/transformer.
column15: Escalate immediately for burning smell or smoke.

column5: Check oil containment bund condition and drains.
column15: Escalate for active spill or overflow risk.

column5: Check for external hot spots/discoloration on panels and terminations.
column15: Escalate for any discoloration or heat indication.

column5: Check transformer oil level and leaks at radiators/valves.
column15: Escalate for low level or active leak.

column5: Inspect LBS/recloser exterior from ground for cracks, tracking, nests, or loose hardware.
column15: Escalate for tracking marks, cracked insulators, or loose parts.

column5: Observe overhead lines for sag changes, broken strands, insulator damage, pole tilt, or guy wire slack.
column15: Escalate for damaged conductors, severe sag, or pole instability.

column5: Record key readings from meters/SCADA (feeder current, transformer load %, voltage).
column15: Escalate for overload or abnormal voltage.

column5: Tighten or replace door seals, latches, and non-energized external hardware where permitted.
column15: Escalate if integrity cannot be restored.

column5: Keep motor area clean and dry; remove standing water or oil; clear ventilation inlets/outlets.
column15: Escalate for water ingress or repeated contamination.

column5: Clean external cooling fins and fan cover screens.
column15: Escalate if cooling path cannot be restored.

column5: For MV motors, keep around cable terminations externally clean and dry.
column15: Escalate for moisture or contamination.

column5: Verify motor load (current/kW) vs baseline.
column15: Escalate if abnormal loading is observed.

column5: For VFD motors, confirm VFD status normal, fans running, and speed setpoint intended.
column15: Escalate for repeated trips/alarms.

column5: Confirm guards and coupling covers are secure; report rubbing noise.
column15: Escalate for loose guards or rubbing.

column5: Smell for burning insulation/ozone; listen for arcing buzz (do not open).
column15: Escalate immediately for electrical burning smell or arcing.

column5: Check unusual external heat by brief touch/scan if safe.
column15: Escalate for high external heat.

column5: Confirm cable glands/conduits intact and no water ingress path.
column15: Escalate for damage or water ingress.

column5: Listen for abnormal noise (bearing, fan, electrical hum increase).
column15: Escalate for new abnormal noise.

column5: Feel vibration at DE/NDE.
column15: Escalate for increased vibration.

column5: Measure bearing housing temperatures (DE/NDE) by IR vs baseline.
column15: Escalate if temperature is high.

column5: Check grease purge condition for excess or contamination.
column15: Escalate for contaminated purge or over-greasing signs.

column5: For MV motors, verify space heater status and condensation risk.
column15: Escalate for condensation or heater failure.

column5: Tighten minor external fasteners on fan cover, nameplates, or junction box covers if permitted.
column15: Escalate if looseness recurs.

column5: Replace damaged coupling guards/fasteners like-for-like.
column15: Escalate if guard safety cannot be restored.

column5: Clean polymer, sludge, and filtrate spills; keep floors dry and anti-slip mats in place.
column15: Escalate for recurring spills or unsafe floor condition.

column5: Flush or verify wash nozzles spray pattern; clean spray bars and guards.
column15: Escalate if spray coverage is poor or nozzle remains blocked.

column5: For belt systems, clean scrapers and filtrate trays; remove solids build-up.
column15: Escalate if buildup quickly recurs.

column5: For filter press, clean drip trays and filtrate channels; remove cake build-up.
column15: Escalate if tray/channel blockage persists.

column5: For decanter systems, clean around skid and wipe oil mist/dust around gearbox housing.
column15: Escalate for repeat oil mist or leak.

column5: Verify sludge feed rate and polymer dose setpoints are within recipe; record overrides.
column15: Escalate if stable operation cannot be maintained.

column5: For belt systems, adjust belt tracking per SOP when drift is observed.
column15: Escalate if tracking cannot be corrected.

column5: For screw/volute, adjust backpressure setpoint if permitted to maintain cake dryness.
column15: Escalate if dryness cannot be achieved without overload.

column5: For filter press, confirm hydraulic closure pressure and cycle parameters match approved recipe.
column15: Escalate if parameters drift from recipe.

column5: Verify guards and interlocks in place on belt, screw, and centrifuge covers.
column15: Escalate if any guard or interlock is not functional.

column5: For filter press, check E-stops and light curtain/door interlocks per SOP.
column15: Escalate for any failed safety device.

column5: For decanter, check abnormal noise/vibration and confirm exclusion zone signage present.
column15: Escalate immediately for severe vibration/noise.

column5: Confirm wash hoses/nozzles intact and polymer lines not leaking.
column15: Escalate for uncontrolled spray or leak.

column5: Record cake dryness/visual condition or %DS where available.
column15: Escalate if product quality falls outside acceptable range.

column5: Observe centrate/filtrate clarity.
column15: Escalate for sustained cloudy filtrate or turbidity alarm.

column5: For belt systems, check roller noise/vibration and belt tension/condition.
column15: Escalate for belt damage or severe vibration.

column5: For filter press, inspect cloth condition and plate edges/gaskets.
column15: Escalate for damaged cloth or leaking plates.

column5: For screw/volute, check screen/volute blinding and wash effectiveness.
column15: Escalate if blinding persists.

column5: For decanter, record vibration trend, oil level/condition, and bearing/gearbox temperatures.
column15: Escalate for high vibration, low oil, or overheating.

column5: Record motor current or kW vs baseline for same throughput.
column15: Escalate for overload or abnormal low load.

column5: Tighten minor external fasteners on covers, guards, and brackets.
column15: Escalate if loosening recurs.

column5: For belt systems, replace or adjust scraper blades like-for-like.
column15: Escalate if performance remains poor.

column5: For filter systems, replace minor leaking hose/fitting on filtrate or drip lines where permitted.
column15: Escalate if leak persists.

column5: For screw/volute, replace blocked wash nozzle tips and clean strainers per SOP.
column15: Escalate if blockage quickly returns.

column5: Wipe control cabinet exteriors; remove dust at vents; ensure doors close and seal.
column15: Escalate if cabinet sealing is compromised.

column5: Clean HMI screens, keyboards, and workstation surfaces using approved method.
column15: Escalate if screens are damaged or unreadable.

column5: Clean around UPS and battery room area; keep floor dry and clear.
column15: Escalate for wet floor or obstruction.

column5: Clean around pneumatic FRL/regulator panels; ensure drains are accessible.
column15: Escalate for blocked drains or contamination.

column5: Verify UPS mode and acknowledge alarms per SOP; avoid repeated resets.
column15: Escalate for bypass, battery operation, or recurring alarms.

column5: Verify critical PLC/SCADA loops are in AUTO; document manual overrides.
column15: Escalate if critical loops remain in manual unexpectedly.

column5: Drain FRL bowls/auto-drains if permitted and verify regulator pressure setpoint.
column15: Escalate if auto-drain fails or pressure is unstable.

column5: Check for burning smell, smoke, or unusual cabinet heat without opening panels.
column15: Escalate immediately for smoke or burning smell.

column5: Verify UPS battery room ventilation and temperature are within range.
column15: Escalate for high temperature or ventilation failure.

column5: Check for uncontrolled pneumatic actuator movement/chatter; confirm barriers/guards in place.
column15: Escalate if unsafe actuator behavior is observed.

column5: Confirm SCADA time sync and alarm banner are functioning; no blank screens on critical HMIs.
column15: Escalate if alarming/display is impaired.

column5: Check PLC cabinet indicators (CPU RUN/FAULT, PSU OK, comms OK).
column15: Escalate for any FAULT or lost comms.

column5: Verify SCADA communications health and network switch link indicators.
column15: Escalate for persistent comms loss.

column5: Record UPS input/output voltage, load %, battery status, and event log summary.
column15: Escalate for battery alarm or abnormal load.

column5: Check cabinet environment for water ingress, condensation, dust mats, fan noise, and filter clogging.
column15: Escalate for moisture ingress or poor cooling.

column5: Inspect pneumatics for air leaks, tubing rubbing, FRL bowl cracks, and pressure stability.
column15: Escalate for active air leaks or unstable pressure.

column5: Observe valve/actuator response for hunting, stiction, or slow movement where safe.
column15: Escalate for degraded response affecting control.

column5: Replace or secure cabinet door gaskets/fasteners; tighten loose external hardware.
column15: Escalate if cabinet sealing cannot be restored.

column5: Replace pneumatic tubing/fittings for minor external leaks if permitted.
column15: Escalate if leakage persists.

column5: Replace HMI peripherals and reseat external cables where safe.
column15: Escalate if device remains non-functional.

column5: Clean around genset skid; remove oil/coolant drips; keep floor dry and clear of combustibles.
column15: Escalate for pooled fuel, oil, or coolant.

column5: Clean radiator air inlet/outlet screens and fins externally.
column15: Escalate if cooling air path is obstructed or damaged.

column5: Wipe control panel exterior; ensure labels and emergency stop are visible.
column15: Escalate if controls/labels are unreadable.

column5: Verify genset mode selector per SOP (AUTO for standby); confirm no bypass active.
column15: Escalate if unit is not in required mode.

column5: Top up day tank to target level per SOP.
column15: Escalate if fuel level cannot be restored.

column5: Drain water from fuel water separator if fitted.
column15: Escalate if water repeatedly accumulates.

column5: Check fire extinguisher in area is accessible and serviceable.
column15: Escalate if extinguisher is missing or not serviceable.

column5: Confirm guards/covers are installed and secured.
column15: Escalate if any guard is missing or loose.

column5: Check battery area condition and charger status.
column15: Escalate for swelling, leaks, or charger alarm.

column5: Listen for abnormal engine noise during run/test.
column15: Escalate for knocking, grinding, or persistent squeal.

column5: Check exhaust smoke during start and under load.
column15: Escalate for sustained black, white, or blue smoke.

column5: Record oil pressure, coolant temperature, voltage, frequency, kW, PF, and alarms.
column15: Escalate for alarm/trip or out-of-range operating values.

column5: Check for fuel, oil, or coolant leaks and quantify drip rate/location.
column15: Escalate for any fuel leak or active stream leak.

column5: Check vibration by feel at skid and alternator bearings during run.
column15: Escalate for sudden increase or severe vibration.

column5: Tighten accessible external clamps and brackets on exhaust flex and radiator screens.
column15: Escalate if repeated loosening is observed.

column5: Replace cracked/loose battery terminal covers or corroded clamps like-for-like.
column15: Escalate if overheating/corrosion recurs.

column5: Replace minor hoses/lines only if authorized and low-risk.
column15: Escalate if replacement is outside operator authority or leak persists.
